zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

filed_by: claude-orchestrator at client direction (response 'A' = Option A Demo MVP)
trigger: Gap-analysis review by client + product-leader perspective revealed original scope (all 14 surfaces) is not multi-day work. Client elected Option A from a 3-option re-cut presented in prior response.

amendment.from_state: Full clone — all 14 wealth.com surfaces, 19 integrations, full Ester, full Tax Planning, all trust strategies (GRAT/SLAT/ILIT/CLAT/Dynasty/Revocable/QPRT/IDGT/CRT/CRUT), Family Office Suite, Heritage Map, Ownership Balance Sheet, Estate Tax Projections.
amendment.to_state: Demo MVP — narrowed to: Auth+Tenant+Identity (Slice 1), Households+People+Entities (Slice 2), Documents+WORM Audit (Slice 3), Ester-substitute extractor with deterministic-calc layer (Slice 4), ONE estate-tax baseline calc under OBBBA $15M permanent (Slice 5 narrowed), ONE scenario modeled (e.g., GRAT vs do-nothing under $15M permanent — Slice 6 narrowed to 1-of-9 strategies), minimal walkable UI for advisor flow (Slice 9), transfer test on a different fintech surface, retrospective. Slices 7 (Reports) and 8 (Integrations Hub beyond 2 mocks) are pulled out of Demo MVP scope.

in_scope_demo_mvp: Multi-tenant data model with Postgres RLS row-level isolation
Auth: SSO-stub + RBAC + MFA-stub (real implementation deferred to post-MVP)
Household → People → Entities (incl. Trust types) with relationships
Document upload + S3-equivalent storage + WORM audit log (immutable append-only)

Ester-substitute extractor: layout-aware OCR mock + LLM extraction + deterministic-calc validation layer + citation grounding (architecture as ADR; implementation as one extraction flow on a sample will/trust document)
Federal estate tax calculation engine for ONE scenario: assets → DSUE → exemption (OBBBA $15M permanent) → taxable estate → 40% top rate → projected tax liability. Golden dataset from public corpora.
ONE scenario modeled in the Scenario Builder: GRAT vs do-nothing for a $20M household under OBBBA. Side-by-side comparison.
Minimal advisor UI: login → dashboard → household list → household detail → document upload → extraction review → estate-tax baseline view → ONE scenario comparison view. Playwright-evidenced.
Transfer test: apply factory to a Holistiplan-style mini-app (single-input tax-baseline calc surface) to validate factory generalization.
Retrospective + skill-system update PRs (lesson L-001 already drafted; will be applied this iteration).
Final Hawkeye conformance audit at run closure.
out_of_scope_demo_mvp_now_deferred: Estate Tax Projections (multi-scenario forward modeling beyond 1 scenario)
Full Tax Planning surface (1040/W-2 ingestion, look-back/forward, prior-year safe harbor)
Document Creation surface (POA, Revocable Trust, AHCD, Guardian Nomination drafting) — too UPL-sensitive for MVP and adds attorney-of-record workflow complexity. Deferred. ADR will note constraint.
Full Scenario Builder strategy library (SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, QPRT, IDGT, CRT/CRUT) — only GRAT included.
Reports surface (Executive Summary, Standalone Visual Report, Form 706/709 worksheets)
Integrations Hub beyond 2 adapters (only Plaid-shape and Schwab-shape contract+mock; the other 17 named in PRD are pulled out)
Voice-to-text intake (Tax Planning § 'just say the word')
rationale: Demo MVP exercises every meta-skill at least once (intake → architecture → construction → V&V → transfer-test → retro), produces a walkable demonstration, and runs the transfer test (the actual proof-of-system). Full clone is months of team work — not appropriate for a multi-day governed run. Surface deletions chosen to keep the regulated-AI-fintech archetype's most evidence-heavy paths (extraction, deterministic calc, scenario comparison, multi-tenant isolation, audit log) IN SCOPE; non-evidence-heavy presentation surfaces (Reports, Heritage Map, etc.) deferred.

impact_assessment.scope: narrowed by ~70%; surface count 14→4 (Auth+Households-Entities+Documents+UI), feature count ~30→~12, scenario count 9→1, integration count 19→2.
impact_assessment.tokens: Re-budget revised: low ~250K, mid ~600K, high ~1M. Within the originally approved CHK-2 mid-band (400K) at risk; will trigger CHK-2.5 spend-pace check at 75% of original mid (300K). Reapproval pre-authorized via 'A' but will checkpoint if mid band exceeded.
impact_assessment.schedule: Multi-day still applies. Estimated 30-45 hr orchestrator + subagent work distributed across multiple sessions.
impact_assessment.quality: RALPH 5-loop discipline RETAINED for in-scope artifacts. Threshold reductions NOT permitted.
impact_assessment.risk: Reduced — narrowed scope reduces failure surface area. New risk: 'demo-only sufficient?' — mitigated by transfer-test bead which tests factory generalization independent of demo polish.

approval.notes: Single-letter 'A' response = Option A acceptance. Reapproval pre-authorized for CC-001.

reapproval_triggers_after_this_change: +15% delta from new revised mid band (600K)
child_beads_unblocked_or_created: TB-WC-2026-0503-CC-PATCH — apply this CC to meta-attractor + ENG-GOV + product-tailoring + SDLC matrix in a single sweep.
TB-WC-2026-0503-RETAG — re-tag PRD atoms with OBBBA + Demo-MVP scope status.
TB-WC-2026-0503-L001 — apply L-001 PRs to skill system.
TB-WC-2026-0426-005 — Stage-1 intake (now narrowed to Demo-MVP atoms only).
